The Lucky One (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $60,457,138Details
International Box Office $36,176,695
Worldwide Box Office $96,633,833
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $26,884,833
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $4,668,250
Total $31,553,083
Further financial details...

Synopsis

U.S. Marine Sergeant Logan Thibault returns from his third tour of duty in Iraq, with the one thing he credits with keeping him alive - a photograph he found of a woman he doesn't even know. Discovering her name is Beth and where she lives, he shows up at her door, and ends up taking a job at her family-run local kennel. Despite her initial mistrust and the complications in her life, a romance develops between them, giving Logan hope that Beth could be much more than his good luck charm.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$22,518,358 (37.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.68 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:62.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,155 opening theaters/3,175 max. theaters, 5.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $90,837,611

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 20th, 2012 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: April 19th, 2012 (Wide) (Slovenia)

Video Release: August 28th, 2012 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some sexuality and violence.
(Rating bulletin 2174, 6/1/2011)
Running Time: 81 minutes
Plot point: Relationships Gone Wrong, Returning Soldiers, Romance
Social setting: Iraq War
Subgenre: Romantic Drama
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Warner Bros., Village Roadshow Productions, Di Novi Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

U.S. Marine Sergeant Logan Thibault returns from his third tour of duty in Iraq, with the one thing he credits with keeping him alive - a photograph he found of a woman he doesn't even know. Discovering her name is Beth and where she lives, he shows up at her door, and ends up taking a job at her family-run local kennel. Despite her initial mistrust and the complications in her life, a romance develops between them, giving Logan hope that Beth could be much more than his good luck charm.
